question: Is it okay to start the course after it has already begun, and what do I need to do if I want the certificate?
answer_agent:
Yes — you can follow the course after it has already started, but **to get a certificate you need to finish with the live cohort**.

A few key points:
- **Self-paced mode does not qualify for a certificate.**
- To earn the certificate, you must **complete the course during a live run** and **participate in the peer-review process**.
- The course requires you to **peer-review 3 capstone projects** after you submit your own project.
- You can only do those peer reviews **while the course is running**; once the review form closes, it’s no longer possible.

If you want, I can also explain what counts as “finishing with the live cohort” in practice.

question: Where do students actually join the Office Hours live stream if the Zoom link isn’t shared with us?
answer_agent:
Students don’t join via the Zoom link directly. For Office Hours/live workshop sessions, the Zoom link is only for instructors/presenters/TAs.

Students should:
- watch on **YouTube Live**
- use **Slido** to submit questions
- check the **announcements channel on Telegram/Slack** for the live video URL before it starts

You can also watch on the DataTalksClub YouTube channel.
answer_orig:
The zoom link is only published to instructors/presenters/TAs.

Students participate via YouTube Live and submit questions to Slido (link is pinned in the chat when live). The video URL should be posted in the [announcements channel on Telegram and Slack](https://t.me/dezoomcamp) before it begins. You can also watch live on the DataTalksClub [YouTube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/c/DataTalksClub).

Don’t post questions in chat as they may be missed if the room is very active.

question: How are we supposed to ask questions during live workshop or office hours sessions?
answer_agent:
During live workshop or office hours sessions, students should:

question: What’s the best place to begin LLM Zoomcamp, and what docs or repo should I check first?
answer_agent:
The best place to begin is:

1. **LLM Zoomcamp docs**  
   https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/

2. **LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repo**  
   https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp

3. **General Zoomcamp logistics docs**  
   https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/

If you want the most practical starting point, **use the GitHub repo first**—it’s the main entry point and is usually kept more up to date than the video playlist. Then follow the lesson folders there, watch the linked videos, and check the homework instructions in the repo.

If you’re tracking deadlines/progress, use the course platform:
https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/

A typical workflow is:
- Watch the lesson videos
- Work through the notebooks/code
- Read homework instructions on GitHub
- Submit on the course platform
answer_orig:
Start with the [LLM Zoomcamp docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/llm-zoomcamp/), the [general Zoomcamp logistics docs](https://datatalks.club/docs/courses/zoomcamp-logistics/), and the [LLM Zoomcamp GitHub repository](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/llm-zoomcamp).

You can start whenever you want. The videos and GitHub materials are available, and the deadlines are listed in the [course management platform](https://courses.datatalks.club/llm-zoomcamp-2026/).

A typical workflow is:

1. Watch the lesson videos.
2. Work through the lesson notebooks/code.
3. Read the homework instructions on GitHub.
4. Submit answers through the course platform before the deadline.

Homework is similar to the lesson flow, but uses a different dataset or slightly different task.

question: How do I find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t spot it right away?
answer_agent:
If you can’t spot your name on the leaderboard, click the **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”** link — it will take you straight to your entry.

Also, when you set up your account, you were assigned a random display name by default. If you want to check or change it, go to **“Edit Course Profile”**:
- **First field:** your public nickname/display name
- **Second field:** your official name for the certificate

If you want, I can also explain how to change your display name.
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!
tool_calls: [{'name': 'search', 'arguments': '{"query":"find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t spot it right away"}'}]

question: Why does my leaderboard name look random, and can I change it to something else?
answer_agent:
Your leaderboard name is random by default because the course automatically assigns you a nickname, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.”

Yes, you can change it:
- Go to **Edit Course Profile**
- The **first field** is your public display name / nickname for the leaderboard
- You can set it to your Slack username, GitHub username, or any nickname you want

If you also want the name on your certificate to be different, that’s the **second field** in the profile, and it should be your official name as in your ID documents.
